documents.title: Daily Drilling Report - Eagle Point 1H - Day 23
documents.content:
DAILY DRILLING REPORT

Well: Eagle Point 1H (EP-001-2024)
Date: March 15, 2024
Status: Drilling Ahead
Current Depth: 8,425 ft MD / 7,890 ft TVD
Formation: Wolfcamp Formation (tight sandstone/shale)

DRILLING OPERATIONS:
Drilled 287 ft in 24 hours using 8.5" PDC bit #3. Maintained WOB at 25-30K lbs with rotary speed of 120-140 RPM. Penetration rate averaged 11.9 ft/hr. Encountered minor torque variations between 7,950-8,100 ft indicating possible fault zone. No significant shows observed.

MUD PROPERTIES:
Mud weight: 11.2 ppg, maintained within target range
Viscosity: 48 sec/qt (Marsh Funnel)
Fluid loss: 6.2 ml/30 min
PH: 9.8
Chlorides: 1,850 ppm
Solids content: 8.4%
Mud volume: 650 bbls active system

BIT PERFORMANCE:
Current bit has 47.3 rotating hours with good cutting structure condition. Dull grade estimated at T-2-I-E-I-2-TD. No junk in hole. Recommend continuing with current bit for next 200-300 ft based on formation consistency.

FORMATION CHARACTERISTICS:
Wolfcamp Formation showing typical tight sandstone interbedded with organic-rich shale layers. Cuttings analysis indicates 15-20% quartz, 45% clay minerals, 25% carbonate cement. Gamma ray readings steady at 85-95 API units. No hydrocarbon shows detected in current interval.

SAFETY & ENVIRONMENTAL:
No safety incidents or near-misses reported. H2S levels remain below detection limits (<5 ppm). Waste management protocols followed with 12 bbls of contaminated mud properly contained. All crew attended morning safety meeting focusing on tong safety procedures.

documents.title: Daily Drilling Report - Bakken Star 3H - Day 41
documents.content:
DAILY DRILLING REPORT

Well: Bakken Star 3H (BKS-003-2024)
Date: March 15, 2024
Status: Drilling Ahead - Lateral Section
Current Depth: 12,890 ft MD / 9,245 ft TVD
Formation: Bakken Formation (organic shale with tight oil zones)

DRILLING OPERATIONS:
Completed 195 ft of lateral drilling in Bakken Formation using 6.125" rotary steerable system. Maintained inclination at 89.5-90.2 degrees with excellent directional control. Drilling parameters optimized: 15-18K lbs WOB, 180 RPM, 280 GPM flow rate. ROP averaged 8.3 ft/hr in challenging shale sections.

MUD PROPERTIES:
Oil-based mud system performing excellently:
Mud weight: 10.8 ppg
Plastic viscosity: 28 cp
Yield point: 18 lb/100ft²
Oil/water ratio: 85/15
Electrical stability: 485 volts
Filtrate: 3.2 ml
Low gravity solids: 4.1%

BIT PERFORMANCE:
PDC bit #7 showing excellent performance in Bakken shale with 38.2 hours runtime. Cutting structure remains sharp with minimal wear on gauge pads. Torque response smooth at 8-12K ft-lbs. MSE calculations indicate optimal drilling efficiency. Bit projected to drill additional 400-500 ft.

FORMATION CHARACTERISTICS:
Bakken Formation exhibiting classic characteristics with TOC values estimated 8-12%. Cuttings show dark brown to black organic-rich shale with occasional calcite stringers. Strong oil shows observed with bright yellow fluorescence under UV light. Gas chromatography indicates C1-C5 hydrocarbon presence. Formation pressure gradient estimated at 0.68 psi/ft.

SAFETY & ENVIRONMENTAL:
Zero incidents reported. Continuous H2S monitoring with levels at 15-25 ppm, well below action limits. Enhanced ventilation systems operating properly. Completed weekly BOP test with all functions operational. Environmental compliance maintained with proper cuttings management.

documents.title: Daily Drilling Report - Cherokee Ridge 2 - Day 18
documents.content:
DAILY DRILLING REPORT

Well: Cherokee Ridge 2 (CHR-002-2024)
Date: March 15, 2024
Status: Drilling Ahead - Intermediate Hole
Current Depth: 6,750 ft MD / 6,745 ft TVD
Formation: Eagle Ford Group (calcareous shale)

DRILLING OPERATIONS:
Drilled 312 ft through Eagle Ford Group using 9.875" tricone bit. Encountered hard limestone stringers requiring increased WOB to 35-40K lbs. Rotary speed maintained at 90-110 RPM. Penetration rate varied 8-16 ft/hr depending on lithology. Conducted wiper trip from 6,450-6,750 ft with minimal drag, indicating good hole condition.

MUD PROPERTIES:
Water-based polymer mud system:
Mud weight: 9.4 ppg
Funnel viscosity: 42 sec/qt
Fluid loss: 8.8 ml/30 min
PH: 10.2
Alkalinity: 0.8/2.3 (Pf/Mf)
Calcium: 180 ppm
Total hardness: 220 ppm
Active mud volume: 580 bbls

BIT PERFORMANCE:
Tricone bit #4 performance degraded in hard Eagle Ford carbonates. Penetration rate dropped 40% over last 8 hours. Torque increased to 18-22K ft-lbs with erratic response. Dull grade estimated T-4-I-A-X-3-TD. Recommend bit trip at current depth to maintain drilling efficiency.

FORMATION CHARACTERISTICS:
Eagle Ford Group showing mixed lithology: 60% calcareous shale, 30% limestone, 10% marl. Cuttings analysis reveals high carbonate content (45-55%) with moderate organic matter. Formation competent with minimal cavings. Slight oil staining observed on limestone cuttings. Gamma ray readings 75-120 API with peaks corresponding to organic-rich intervals.

SAFETY & ENVIRONMENTAL:
Safety performance excellent with zero recordable incidents. Conducted weekly safety meeting on well control procedures. All personnel current on H2S certification. Mud logging trailer H2S detection system tested and calibrated. Waste volume: 8 bbls oil-contaminated materials properly segregated for disposal. Environmental monitoring shows no impact to surrounding area.

documents.title: Daily Drilling Report - Wolfcamp Express 5H - Day 35
documents.content:
DAILY DRILLING REPORT

Well: Wolfcamp Express 5H (WCE-005-2024)
Date: March 15, 2024
Status: Drilling Ahead - Curve Section
Current Depth: 9,125 ft MD / 8,890 ft TVD
Formation: Bone Spring Limestone (limestone/dolomite)

DRILLING OPERATIONS:
Building angle through Bone Spring Limestone using motor assembly with 1.5° bend. Current inclination: 67.2°, building at 3.8°/100ft. Drilled 156 ft with excellent directional control. Motor parameters: 40 GPM flow rate, 300 PSI differential pressure. Slide/rotate ratio 70/30 to maintain trajectory. No significant drilling problems encountered.

MUD PROPERTIES:
Synthetic-based mud (SBM) system:
Mud weight: 12.1 ppg (elevated for pressure management)
Plastic viscosity: 35 cp
Yield point: 23 lb/100ft²
Gel strengths: 8/15 lb/100ft² (10 sec/10 min)
Filtrate: 2.8 ml
Retort: 78% oil, 16% water, 6% solids
Electrical stability: 520 volts

BIT PERFORMANCE:
PDC bit #5 performing well in hard Bone Spring carbonates. 42.8 hours on bit with good cutting structure integrity. WOB maintained at 20-25K lbs with smooth torque response 10-14K ft-lbs. ROP averaged 6.7 ft/hr, consistent with offset wells in similar formation. Bit condition estimated 3-4 on IADC scale.

FORMATION CHARACTERISTICS:
Bone Spring Limestone composed primarily of dense limestone (70%) and dolomite (25%) with minor shale interbeds (5%). Formation very competent with minimal hole enlargement. Cuttings exhibit light gray to tan coloration with crystalline texture. Minor oil shows observed with pale yellow fluorescence. Formation pressure slightly overpressured at 0.52 psi/ft gradient.

SAFETY & ENVIRONMENTAL:
Outstanding safety record continues with 127 days without incident. All safety systems operational including BOP stack tested at 5,000 PSI. H2S levels consistently below 10 ppm. Completed monthly environmental inspection with no findings. SBM recovery system operating at 92% efficiency, minimizing waste generation.

documents.title: Daily Drilling Report - Gulf Stream 1 - Day 12
documents.content:
DAILY DRILLING REPORT

Well: Gulf Stream 1 (GS-001-2024)
Date: March 15, 2024
Status: Drilling Ahead - Surface Hole
Current Depth: 3,850 ft MD / 3,850 ft TVD
Formation: Marcellus Shale (organic-rich shale)

DRILLING OPERATIONS:
Drilled 425 ft through upper Marcellus Shale using 12.25" PDC bit. Excellent drilling conditions with consistent formation properties. Maintained drilling parameters: WOB 22-28K lbs, rotary speed 80-100 RPM, flow rate 520 GPM. Penetration rate averaged 35.4 ft/hr, exceeding AFE estimates by 15%. No wellbore stability issues observed.

MUD PROPERTIES:
Freshwater polymer mud system:
Mud weight: 9.0 ppg
Funnel viscosity: 38 sec/qt
Yield point: 12 lb/100ft²
Fluid loss: 12.4 ml/30 min
PH: 9.6
Chlorides: 420 ppm
Potassium: 1,200 ppm (KCl addition for shale stability)
Solids: 6.2%
Active volume: 720 bbls

BIT PERFORMANCE:
PDC bit #2 demonstrating exceptional performance in Marcellus Shale. Only 28.5 hours on bit with all cutters intact and sharp. Minimal wear on gauge protection with smooth drilling torque 6-9K ft-lbs. Hydraulic horsepower optimized at 2.8 HP/in². Bit capable of drilling to planned casing point at 4,500 ft.

FORMATION CHARACTERISTICS:
Marcellus Shale exhibiting typical characteristics: dark gray to black fissile shale with high organic content (8-15% TOC). Cuttings analysis shows 65% clay minerals, 25% quartz, 8% pyrite, 2% carbonate. Strong hydrocarbon shows with bright white fluorescence and rapid gas liberation. Background gas 180-220 units increasing. Natural fractures observed with calcite mineralization.

SAFETY & ENVIRONMENTAL:
Excellent safety performance with proactive hazard identification during daily meetings. H2S monitoring systems fully operational with levels <5 ppm. Completed BOP drill achieving connection time of 4 minutes 30 seconds. Environmental monitoring shows no impact to local water sources. Cuttings management plan executed with 15 bbls waste properly contained and staged for licensed disposal facility.

documents.title: Safety Inspection Report - Eagle Point 1H Well Site
documents.content:
SAFETY INSPECTION REPORT

Well Name: Eagle Point 1H
Inspector: Michael Rodriguez, Certified Safety Professional
Inspection Type: Quarterly Safety & Environmental Assessment
Inspection Date: October 15, 2024
Result: PASS with Minor Deficiencies

FINDINGS:
Conducted comprehensive safety inspection of Eagle Point 1H well site and associated equipment. The Lufkin C-456D Pump Jack showed proper operational parameters with all safety guards in place. However, observed minor hydraulic fluid leak at base requiring immediate attention. The 300 BBL Production Tank exhibited proper secondary containment with no visible signs of corrosion or structural integrity issues.

Environmental assessment revealed compliance with current air quality standards. Noise levels measured at 68 dB at property boundary, well within regulatory limits per Permit 2 specifications. No evidence of soil contamination or groundwater impact detected during visual inspection.

REGULATORY COMPLIANCE:
Site maintains full compliance with EPA regulations 40 CFR Part 60 and state environmental standards. All required safety signage present and legible. Emergency response equipment including eyewash station and spill kits properly maintained and within expiration dates.

CORRECTIVE ACTIONS REQUIRED:
1. Repair hydraulic fluid leak on pump jack within 72 hours
2. Replace worn safety barrier tape around wellhead area
3. Update emergency contact information on site notification board

FOLLOW-UP SCHEDULE:
Re-inspection scheduled for November 1, 2024, to verify completion of corrective actions. Next quarterly inspection due January 15, 2025. Site operator notified of all findings and acknowledged responsibility for timely completion of required actions.

Inspector Signature: M. Rodriguez
Date: October 15, 2024

documents.title: Environmental Compliance Inspection - Bakken Star 3H
documents.content:
FIELD SAFETY & ENVIRONMENTAL INSPECTION REPORT

Well Identification: Bakken Star 3H
Lead Inspector: Sarah Chen, Environmental Compliance Specialist
Inspection Classification: Annual Environmental Impact Assessment
Inspection Date: September 28, 2024
Overall Result: CONDITIONAL PASS

DETAILED FINDINGS:
Performed thorough environmental and safety evaluation of Bakken Star 3H production facility. The Ariel JGK Gas Compressor demonstrated excellent maintenance standards with zero fugitive emissions detected during leak detection survey using OGI camera. All pressure relief valves tested within acceptable parameters.

Identified significant concern with 16-inch Gathering Pipeline showing signs of external corrosion at mile marker 2.3. Cathodic protection system readings indicate potential coating failure requiring immediate engineering assessment. Soil sampling conducted around affected area showed no hydrocarbon contamination at this time.

Water management practices observed to be exemplary. Produced water handling system operating efficiently with proper disposal documentation maintained. Stormwater management compliant with NPDES permit requirements.

COMPLIANCE STATUS:
Partial compliance noted. Site operating under Permit 3 authorization with minor deviations requiring corrective action. OSHA safety standards fully met with proper PPE availability and confined space entry procedures documented.

MANDATORY CORRECTIVE ACTIONS:
1. Initiate pipeline integrity assessment within 30 days
2. Implement enhanced corrosion monitoring program
3. Submit pipeline repair timeline to regulatory authority
4. Install additional soil monitoring wells at pipeline crossing

FOLLOW-UP REQUIREMENTS:
Progress report due November 15, 2024. Full re-inspection scheduled upon completion of pipeline repairs. Operator must provide weekly status updates during remediation period.

Inspector Authorization: S. Chen, CEM #4782
Report Date: September 28, 2024

documents.title: Routine Safety Inspection Report - Cherokee Ridge 2
documents.content:
SAFETY INSPECTION DOCUMENTATION

Facility: Cherokee Ridge 2 Well Site
Inspecting Officer: James Thompson, Safety Compliance Manager
Inspection Category: Monthly Routine Safety Check
Date of Inspection: November 3, 2024
Inspection Result: SATISFACTORY

INSPECTION SUMMARY:
Completed monthly safety inspection of Cherokee Ridge 2 production site. All primary safety systems functioning properly with no immediate hazards identified. The Natco 3-Phase Separator operating within design parameters showing excellent separation efficiency and proper pressure control.

Fire suppression systems tested successfully with all portable extinguishers showing proper pressure and recent inspection tags. Fixed fire suppression system around separator vessel activated during test sequence without issues. Emergency shutdown systems responded appropriately during functional testing.

Personnel safety protocols observed during inspection showed good adherence to established procedures. Confined space entry permits properly completed for tank inspection activities. Gas monitoring equipment calibrated and functional.

REGULATORY ADHERENCE:
Full compliance maintained with DOT pipeline safety regulations and state oil and gas commission requirements. Current operating permits (Permit 4) remain valid through March 2025. Safety data sheets current and accessible. Training records reviewed and found complete.

MINOR OBSERVATIONS:
1. Recommend replacement of weathered safety signage at east gate entrance
2. Suggest additional lighting near separator unit for improved visibility
3. Update emergency evacuation route map to reflect recent site modifications

RECOMMENDATIONS:
No immediate corrective actions required. Suggestions listed above should be addressed during next scheduled maintenance window. Continue current safety practices and monitoring protocols.

NEXT INSPECTION:
Routine monthly inspection scheduled for December 3, 2024. Special focus on winter weather preparedness and freeze protection systems activation.

Certified By: J. Thompson, CSP
Submission Date: November 3, 2024

documents.title: Critical Safety Assessment - Wolfcamp Express 5H
documents.content:
COMPREHENSIVE SAFETY INSPECTION REPORT

Well Designation: Wolfcamp Express 5H
Chief Inspector: Dr. Patricia Williams, Senior Safety Engineer
Inspection Protocol: Semi-Annual Comprehensive Safety Review
Execution Date: August 22, 2024
Final Result: REQUIRES IMMEDIATE ATTENTION

CRITICAL FINDINGS:
Conducted intensive safety evaluation of Wolfcamp Express 5H high-pressure production facility. Discovered significant safety concern with pressure relief valve on 300 BBL Production Tank showing signs of seat leakage and potential failure. Immediate shutdown recommended until valve replacement completed.

H2S monitoring system calibration expired, creating potential exposure risk for personnel. Personal gas monitors tested and found two units non-functional. Site atmosphere testing conducted with portable equipment confirmed safe working conditions at time of inspection.

Electrical systems inspection revealed non-compliant wiring installations in control panel housing. Arc flash protection inadequate per NFPA 70E standards. Grounding system tested satisfactory with proper continuity measurements recorded.

COMPLIANCE DEFICIENCIES:
Multiple violations of OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 Process Safety Management standards identified. Permit 5 conditions not fully met regarding safety equipment maintenance schedules. Immediate regulatory notification required.

MANDATORY CORRECTIVE ACTIONS:
1. Replace defective pressure relief valve before resuming operations
2. Recalibrate all H2S detection equipment immediately
3. Repair/replace faulty personal gas monitors
4. Correct electrical panel deficiencies within 10 days
5. Submit updated Process Hazard Analysis to regulatory authority

ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS:
Production suspension recommended until critical safety items addressed. Regulatory agencies notified of findings. Operator must provide certified completion documentation before restart authorization.

REINSPECTION SCHEDULE:
Mandatory follow-up inspection within 14 days of reported corrections completion.

Authorizing Signature: Dr. P. Williams, PE
Report Generation Date: August 22, 2024

documents.title: Environmental Impact Inspection - Gulf Stream 1
documents.content:
ENVIRONMENTAL & SAFETY COMPLIANCE REPORT

Production Facility: Gulf Stream 1
Certified Inspector: Robert Martinez, Environmental Health & Safety Specialist
Inspection Type: Bi-Annual Environmental Compliance Audit
Date Conducted: July 10, 2024
Assessment Result: EXCEEDS STANDARDS

COMPREHENSIVE EVALUATION:
Performed detailed environmental and safety assessment of Gulf Stream 1 offshore production platform. All safety management systems demonstrated exceptional performance with zero non-conformities identified. Emergency response drills conducted during inspection showed excellent crew preparedness and equipment readiness.

The 16-inch Gathering Pipeline underwater inspection via ROV revealed excellent coating condition with no signs of marine growth interference or structural degradation. Cathodic protection system operating optimally with all readings within design specifications.

Waste management practices exemplary with proper segregation and manifesting of hazardous materials. Produced water discharge monitoring shows consistent compliance with NPDES permit limitations. Marine mammal protection protocols properly implemented with designated observers maintaining required logs.

ENVIRONMENTAL PERFORMANCE:
Air emissions well below permitted limits with continuous monitoring system showing 99.7% uptime. Noise pollution measurements at 500-meter radius indicate full compliance with marine protection standards. No reportable spills or releases during audit period.

BEST PRACTICES OBSERVED:
Site demonstrates industry-leading environmental stewardship. Preventive maintenance program exceeds regulatory requirements. Personnel training records comprehensive and current. Emergency response equipment tested and certified.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
No corrective actions required. Recommend sharing best practices with other facilities in fleet. Consider nomination for environmental excellence recognition program.

CONTINUED MONITORING:
Maintain current inspection schedule. Next comprehensive audit due January 2025. Monthly environmental monitoring reports continue to show consistent compliance trends.

Report Certified By: R. Martinez, CIH
Final Report Date: July 10, 2024

documents.title: Environmental Compliance Report - Permit 1 (Air Quality Permit) - Eagle Point 1H
documents.content: This environmental compliance report is submitted to the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality pursuant to Permit 1 (Air Quality Permit) for well Eagle Point 1H located in Karnes County, Texas. OPERATIONAL SUMMARY: During Q3 2024, Eagle Point 1H maintained continuous production operations with an average daily production rate of 850 BOPD and 2.1 MMCF/D of natural gas. All flaring activities were conducted in accordance with permit conditions, with total flared volume of 45,000 MCF representing 2.1% of total gas production. COMPLIANCE METRICS: NOx emissions averaged 12.4 tons/year, well within the permitted limit of 25 tons/year. VOC emissions totaled 8.7 tons/year against a permitted limit of 15 tons/year. H2S concentrations at the fence line averaged 0.003 ppm, maintaining compliance with the 0.080 ppm limit. ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING DATA: Continuous emissions monitoring systems operated at 99.2% uptime. Monthly leak detection and repair inspections identified and remediated 3 minor fugitive emission sources. Ambient air quality monitoring at the northeast boundary showed PM2.5 levels averaging 12.8 μg/m³, well below NAAQS standards. PERMIT CONDITION STATUS: All 17 permit conditions remain in full compliance. Required quarterly reporting has been submitted timely. Emergency response equipment testing completed with 100% operational status. We certify that all information contained herein is true and accurate to the best of our knowledge.

documents.title: Water Discharge Permit Compliance Filing - Permit 2 (NPDES Permit) - Bakken Star 3H
documents.content: This compliance filing is submitted to the North Dakota Department of Environmental Quality for Permit 2 (NPDES Permit) covering water discharge activities at Bakken Star 3H in McKenzie County, North Dakota. OPERATIONAL SUMMARY: Bakken Star 3H completed hydraulic fracturing operations in August 2024, utilizing 4.2 million gallons of freshwater and generating approximately 1.8 million gallons of flowback water. Current production operations generate an average of 280 barrels of produced water daily, all of which is transported via pipeline to the Williston Water Treatment Facility. COMPLIANCE METRICS: Discharge point effluent quality consistently met all permit limits. Total Dissolved Solids averaged 890 mg/L against a limit of 2,000 mg/L. Oil and grease concentrations remained below detection limits of 5 mg/L throughout the reporting period. pH levels ranged from 7.2 to 8.1, within the permitted range of 6.0-9.0. ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING DATA: Groundwater monitoring wells MW-1 through MW-4 show no statistical increase in chloride, benzene, or other indicator parameters. Surface water sampling at two downstream locations indicates no impact to Tobacco Creek water quality. Monthly biological monitoring confirms healthy aquatic communities with no observable stress indicators. PERMIT CONDITION STATUS: All special conditions are in compliance including the spill prevention plan update and operator certification renewals. Inspection 2 conducted by NDDEQ in September 2024 resulted in no violations or corrective actions. Required annual groundwater assessment submitted on schedule.

documents.title: Drilling Permit Compliance Report - Permit 3 (APD Authorization) - Cherokee Ridge 2
documents.content: This compliance report is filed with the Bureau of Land Management pursuant to Permit 3 (Application for Permit to Drill) for Cherokee Ridge 2 located on federal lease NM-108756 in Eddy County, New Mexico. OPERATIONAL SUMMARY: Cherokee Ridge 2 drilling operations commenced on September 15, 2024, and reached total depth of 8,450 feet on October 22, 2024. The well was drilled using a closed-loop mud system with 100% containment of drill cuttings and fluids. Surface hole was set at 2,100 feet with 13-3/8 inch casing, followed by intermediate casing at 6,200 feet using 9-5/8 inch steel casing. Production casing of 5-1/2 inch was set to total depth with full cement circulation achieved. COMPLIANCE METRICS: All drilling activities maintained 500-foot setback from identified sensitive areas. Noise levels at nearest residence averaged 48 dB, below the 55 dB daytime limit. H2S monitoring equipment operated continuously with maximum detected concentration of 12 ppm, triggering appropriate safety protocols. Zero reportable spills or releases occurred during operations. ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING DATA: Air quality monitoring showed PM10 levels averaging 95 μg/m³ during active drilling, within permitted limits. Cultural resource monitors were present during surface disturbance with no artifacts encountered. Wildlife monitoring documented continued use of adjacent habitat areas by mule deer and pronghorn populations. PERMIT CONDITION STATUS: All 23 permit stipulations have been satisfied including archaeological clearance, biological surveys, and bonding requirements. Inspection 3 by BLM occurred on October 10, 2024, with full compliance noted. Required drilling reports submitted within 30-day timeframes.

documents.title: Production Facility Permit Annual Report - Permit 4 (Operating Permit) - Wolfcamp Express 5H
documents.content: Annual compliance report submitted to the Railroad Commission of Texas for Permit 4 (Operating Permit) covering production facilities at Wolfcamp Express 5H in Reeves County, Texas. OPERATIONAL SUMMARY: Wolfcamp Express 5H achieved first production on March 12, 2024, with current stabilized production rates of 1,250 BOPD and 3.8 MMCF/D. The facility operates a three-phase separator system with 400-barrel oil storage tanks and low-pressure flare system. Gas compression equipment maintains pipeline delivery pressure of 1,200 psig. Produced water handling capacity of 500 BPD utilizes closed-loop trucking for disposal at permitted Class II injection wells. COMPLIANCE METRICS: Facility operated with 98.7% uptime during the reporting period. Emergency shutdown systems tested monthly with 100% functionality. Pressure relief devices calibrated and tested per API standards with all systems within tolerance. Flare efficiency maintained above 98% with proper flame characteristics documented through quarterly inspections. ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING DATA: Quarterly soil sampling at eight perimeter locations shows no hydrocarbon contamination above detection limits. Groundwater monitoring well GW-5H indicates stable water quality with no impact from operations. Vegetation monitoring within 1,000-foot radius shows healthy plant communities with no stress indicators. Air emissions calculations based on actual production data confirm compliance with permitted thresholds. PERMIT CONDITION STATUS: All operational requirements satisfied including operator training, emergency response procedures, and financial assurance. Inspection 4 completed by RRC field inspector resulted in satisfactory rating with no deficiencies. Required production reports filed timely through the P-5 system with accurate allocation data.

documents.title: Offshore Installation Safety Case - Permit 5 (OCS Permit) - Gulf Stream 1
documents.content: Safety Case submission to the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement for Permit 5 (Outer Continental Shelf Permit) covering Gulf Stream 1 operations in Mississippi Canyon Block 252, Gulf of Mexico. OPERATIONAL SUMMARY: Gulf Stream 1 subsea completion was installed in 1,847 feet of water depth in May 2024, tied back to the Thunder Horse platform via 12-inch production flowline. Current production averages 4,200 BOPD with 98% oil cut and minimal water production. Subsea tree and manifold systems operate remotely with real-time monitoring of pressure, temperature, and flow parameters. Integrity management program includes annual subsea inspections and quarterly ROV surveys. COMPLIANCE METRICS: Safety management system maintains 99.1% completion rate for planned maintenance activities. Personnel certification compliance stands at 100% with current BOSIET and helicopter underwater escape training. Emergency response drills conducted quarterly with average response time of 3.2 minutes to muster stations. Pollution prevention equipment tested monthly with 100% operational status of containment systems. ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING DATA: Marine mammal observers documented standard Gulf species with no unusual behavioral patterns noted. Water quality monitoring at four stations shows parameters within natural variability. Seafloor survey data indicates no detectable impact to benthic communities around installation sites. Chemical discharge monitoring confirms compliance with NPDES general permit conditions. PERMIT CONDITION STATUS: All safety case elements remain current including HAZOP studies, quantitative risk assessment, and performance standards verification. Inspection 5 by BSEE resulted in zero incidents of non-compliance. Required monthly production reports submitted through OGOR system with accurate hydrocarbon accounting and royalty calculations.

documents.title: Eagle Point 1H Production Log - Wolfcamp A Lower Bench
documents.content:
Well: Eagle Point 1H
Reservoir: Wolfcamp A Lower Bench (Unconventional Shale)
Daily Production: 245 bbl
Date: November 15, 2024

Production rates show consistent performance with oil production averaging 245 bbl/day over the past 30-day period. Gas production remains steady at 420 Mcf/day with a GOR of 1,714 scf/bbl. Water cut has increased to 28% from 22% recorded last quarter, indicating natural reservoir pressure decline and potential water breakthrough from adjacent formations.

Pressure readings from downhole gauges indicate flowing tubing head pressure of 1,850 psi with casing pressure at 2,100 psi. Static bottom hole pressure measured at 3,200 psi during recent buildup test, showing 15% decline from initial reservoir pressure of 3,765 psi.

Decline curve analysis using Arps hyperbolic model indicates initial decline rate of 45% nominal with b-factor of 1.2, transitioning to exponential decline of 8% annually. Current EUR estimate revised to 185,000 bbl based on 18-month production history.

The Lufkin C-456D Pump Jack system is operating at 12 SPM with 168-inch stroke length, achieving 92% volumetric efficiency. Surface equipment including Natco 3-Phase Separator is handling fluid volumes effectively with minimal operational issues.

Optimization recommendations include implementing gas lift to reduce hydrostatic pressure and increase drawdown. Consider acidizing treatment to improve near-wellbore permeability. Artificial lift redesign may extend economic life by 24 months. Water disposal strategy through existing 16-inch Gathering Pipeline remains viable with current cut levels.

documents.title: Bakken Star 3H Production Log - Bakken Middle Member
documents.content:
Well: Bakken Star 3H
Reservoir: Bakken Middle Member (Unconventional Shale)
Daily Production: 180 bbl
Date: November 15, 2024

Bakken Star 3H demonstrates stable production characteristics with current oil rate of 180 bbl/day, representing controlled decline from initial production of 425 bbl/day. Associated gas production measures 315 Mcf/day yielding GOR of 1,750 scf/bbl, consistent with Bakken Middle Member reservoir fluid properties.

Water production has stabilized at 45 bbl/day representing 20% water cut, within expected parameters for this formation. Chloride analysis indicates formation water with 180,000 ppm total dissolved solids, confirming reservoir source rather than drilling fluid contamination.

Flowing wellhead pressure recorded at 1,200 psi with line pressure maintained at 800 psi through choke management. Recent pressure transient analysis reveals permeability of 0.08 md with skin factor of -2.5, indicating effective hydraulic fracturing completion.

Production decline follows typical Bakken performance with steep initial decline transitioning to more gradual exponential decline. Decline curve modeling projects remaining reserves of 95,000 bbl over 15-year economic limit.

The 300 BBL Production Tank requires emptying every 1.7 days maintaining optimal fluid handling capacity. Ariel JGK Gas Compressor operates efficiently at 75% capacity handling associated gas volumes for sales line delivery.

Recommendations include implementing plunger lift system to improve liquid removal efficiency and reduce tubing pressure. Consider refracturing evaluation after analyzing offset well performance. Monthly production surveillance should continue monitoring water cut trends and pressure maintenance.

documents.title: Cherokee Ridge 2 Production Log - Eagle Ford Black Oil Window
documents.content:
Well: Cherokee Ridge 2
Reservoir: Eagle Ford Black Oil Window (Unconventional Shale)
Daily Production: 315 bbl
Date: November 15, 2024

Cherokee Ridge 2 continues strong production performance with current oil rate of 315 bbl/day from Eagle Ford Black Oil Window completion. The well maintains excellent productivity with associated gas production of 535 Mcf/day, yielding favorable GOR of 1,698 scf/bbl typical of this reservoir window.

Water production remains minimal at 15 bbl/day representing 4.5% water cut, indicating strong reservoir pressure support and effective completion isolation. Produced water salinity measures 140,000 ppm TDS confirming formation source.

Wellhead flowing pressure stabilized at 2,450 psi with separator pressure maintained at 500 psi through surface choke adjustment. Bottom hole flowing pressure estimated at 3,850 psi based on gradient calculations and recent memory gauge data.

Production decline analysis indicates initial exponential decline rate of 35% nominal, moderating to 12% annual decline. Reservoir simulation suggests EUR of 275,000 bbl with current depletion strategy.

Surface facilities perform optimally with Natco 3-Phase Separator achieving 99.5% oil recovery efficiency. The 16-inch Gathering Pipeline maintains adequate capacity for current and projected production volumes through facility expansion.

Optimization opportunities include optimizing choke size to maximize productivity while maintaining reservoir pressure. Consider implementing real-time pressure monitoring system. Evaluate enhanced oil recovery techniques including CO2 injection pilot program. Continue monthly well testing protocol to monitor performance trends and identify any operational anomalies requiring intervention.

documents.title: Wolfcamp Express 5H Production Log - Bone Spring Sand
documents.content:
Well: Wolfcamp Express 5H
Reservoir: Bone Spring Sand (Conventional Sandstone)
Daily Production: 425 bbl
Date: November 15, 2024

Wolfcamp Express 5H delivers robust production from Bone Spring Sand reservoir with current oil rate of 425 bbl/day. This conventional sandstone completion demonstrates excellent reservoir quality with sustained productivity and manageable decline characteristics.

Gas production averages 680 Mcf/day with GOR of 1,600 scf/bbl, consistent with solution gas drive mechanism. Water cut remains low at 12% with production water rate of 58 bbl/day, indicating effective aquifer pressure support without significant water encroachment.

Flowing tubing head pressure measures 1,950 psi with wellhead temperature of 165°F. Static reservoir pressure from recent buildup test indicates 4,200 psi, representing minimal depletion from original pressure of 4,400 psi.

Decline curve analysis shows gentle exponential decline of 18% annually, significantly lower than typical unconventional completions. Reservoir engineering estimates EUR of 485,000 bbl based on material balance calculations and analog performance.

The Lufkin C-456D Pump Jack operates efficiently at 10 SPM with 144-inch stroke, maintaining 95% volumetric efficiency. Surface equipment handles production volumes effectively with the 300 BBL Production Tank providing adequate storage capacity.

Recommendations include maintaining current artificial lift configuration while monitoring for any efficiency degradation. Consider waterflood pilot implementation to enhance recovery factors. Install permanent downhole gauges for real-time pressure monitoring. Evaluate infill drilling opportunities based on reservoir performance data. Continue quarterly pressure transient testing to monitor reservoir connectivity and compartmentalization effects.

documents.title: Gulf Stream 1 Production Log - Marcellus Shale Formation
documents.content:
Well: Gulf Stream 1
Reservoir: Marcellus Shale Formation (Unconventional Shale)
Daily Production: 85 bbl
Date: November 15, 2024

Gulf Stream 1 produces primarily from wet gas window of Marcellus Shale Formation with condensate production of 85 bbl/day. Gas production dominates at 2,450 Mcf/day yielding high GOR of 28,824 scf/bbl, characteristic of gas-condensate reservoir fluid.

Condensate-to-gas ratio remains stable at 35 bbl/MMcf with C5+ content averaging 4.2 gal/Mcf. Water production minimal at 8 bbl/day representing 8.6% water cut, primarily from completion fluids and minor formation water influx.

Wellhead pressure maintained at 3,200 psi with flowing temperature of 145°F. Line pressure regulation through 64/64" choke maintains optimal gas velocity for liquid carryover prevention. Recent pressure buildup indicates reservoir pressure of 4,850 psi.

Decline analysis shows typical shale gas performance with initial decline rate of 55% transitioning to 10% exponential decline. Gas EUR estimated at 4.2 Bcf with condensate recovery of 145,000 bbl over well life.

The Ariel JGK Gas Compressor handles full gas stream at 85% capacity with glycol dehydration maintaining pipeline specifications. Condensate handling through existing infrastructure proves adequate for current production levels.

Recommendations include installing plunger lift system to optimize liquid removal and reduce wellbore loading. Consider velocity string to improve gas lift efficiency. Implement real-time gas analysis to monitor retrograde condensation effects. Evaluate compression requirements for late-life production. Monthly well testing should focus on gas composition changes and pressure maintenance monitoring to optimize long-term recovery strategies.

documents.title: Equipment Maintenance Report - Lufkin C-456D Pump Jack (Unit PJ-001)
documents.content:
EQUIPMENT MAINTENANCE REPORT

Equipment: Lufkin C-456D Pump Jack (Unit PJ-001)
Location: Eagle Point 1H
Equipment Type: Artificial Lift System
Condition: Fair - Requires Immediate Attention
Operating Hours: 4,247 hours
Technician: Sarah Chen
Date: March 15, 2024

WORK PERFORMED:
Conducted comprehensive inspection of pump jack unit following reports of irregular pumping cycles. Replaced worn horsehead bearing assembly and counterweight guide bushings. Performed belt tensioning on prime mover system and adjusted walking beam timing. Rebuilt stuffing box assembly with new packing rings and updated fluid end components.

PARTS REPLACED:
- Horsehead bearing assembly (P/N LFK-4562)
- Counterweight guide bushings (qty 4)
- V-belt set for prime mover
- Stuffing box packing rings
- Fluid end O-ring seals

TEST RESULTS:
Dynamic testing revealed improved load distribution with 15% reduction in peak stress readings. Pumping efficiency increased from 67% to 82%. Surface unit operating temperature reduced by 12°F. Vibration analysis shows acceptable levels across all measurement points. Production rate restored to 45 BOPD baseline.

SAFETY OBSERVATIONS:
Guard rails secure, emergency stops functional. Warning signs properly positioned and legible.

NEXT SERVICE RECOMMENDATION:
Schedule 90-day inspection for June 15, 2024. Monitor production rates weekly. Replace prime mover oil filter at 5,000 operating hours. Consider upgrading to enhanced bearing package during next major overhaul scheduled for Q4 2024.

documents.title: Equipment Maintenance Report - Ariel JGK Gas Compressor (Unit GC-003)
documents.content:
EQUIPMENT MAINTENANCE REPORT

Equipment: Ariel JGK Gas Compressor (Unit GC-003)
Location: Bakken Star 3H
Equipment Type: Reciprocating Gas Compressor
Condition: Good
Operating Hours: 2,156 hours
Technician: Marcus Rodriguez
Date: March 18, 2024

WORK PERFORMED:
Executed scheduled quarterly maintenance on reciprocating compressor system. Performed valve inspection and replacement on cylinder 2. Conducted crankcase oil analysis and full oil change with filter replacement. Cleaned and calibrated pressure relief valves and safety systems. Inspected cylinder liners for wear patterns and performed compression testing across all four cylinders.

PARTS REPLACED:
- Cylinder 2 inlet valve assembly
- Crankcase oil filter cartridge
- Air intake pre-filter elements (qty 2)
- Valve spring retainers
- Gasket kit for valve covers

TEST RESULTS:
Compression test results: Cylinder 1: 485 PSI, Cylinder 2: 492 PSI, Cylinder 3: 478 PSI, Cylinder 4: 483 PSI. All readings within acceptable tolerance of ±5%. Vibration analysis indicates normal operating characteristics. Discharge pressure maintaining 850 PSI with 15% capacity margin. Oil analysis shows acceptable contamination levels with no metal particles detected.

SAFETY OBSERVATIONS:
Emergency shutdown systems tested and verified operational. Gas detection equipment calibrated and functional.

NEXT SERVICE RECOMMENDATION:
Next maintenance due at 2,400 operating hours (estimated June 2024). Replace piston rings during next major overhaul. Monitor cylinder 3 performance closely due to slightly lower compression reading. Schedule intercooler cleaning for next service interval.

documents.title: Equipment Maintenance Report - Natco 3-Phase Separator (Unit SEP-002)
documents.content:
EQUIPMENT MAINTENANCE REPORT

Equipment: Natco 3-Phase Separator (Unit SEP-002)
Location: Cherokee Ridge 2
Equipment Type: Production Separator
Condition: Excellent
Operating Hours: 8,734 hours
Technician: Fatima Al-Rashid
Date: March 20, 2024

WORK PERFORMED:
Completed annual internal inspection following 30-day shutdown for scheduled maintenance. Performed ultrasonic thickness testing on vessel walls and heads. Replaced level control instrumentation and recalibrated all pressure transmitters. Conducted hydrostatic pressure test at 1.5 times maximum allowable working pressure. Cleaned internal demister pads and replaced corroded internal piping supports.

PARTS REPLACED:
- Level control transmitter (LT-201)
- Demister pad assembly
- Internal support brackets (stainless steel)
- Manway gasket and bolting
- Pressure relief valve spring assembly

TEST RESULTS:
Hydrostatic test successful at 450 PSI (MAWP: 300 PSI). Ultrasonic readings confirm vessel wall thickness within design specifications. No evidence of corrosion or material degradation observed. Separation efficiency test achieved 99.2% water removal and 99.8% gas separation. Interface level controls responding accurately within ±0.5 inch tolerance.

SAFETY OBSERVATIONS:
Pressure relief system functioning properly. All safety instrumentation calibrated and operational.

NEXT SERVICE RECOMMENDATION:
Next internal inspection due March 2025. Replace gas outlet valve actuator at next shutdown. Consider upgrading control system to smart transmitters for enhanced monitoring capabilities. Schedule quarterly external visual inspections to monitor for corrosion. Implement weekly interface level trend monitoring to optimize separation performance.

documents.title: Equipment Maintenance Report - 16-inch Gathering Pipeline (Segment GL-004)
documents.content:
EQUIPMENT MAINTENANCE REPORT

Equipment: 16-inch Gathering Pipeline (Segment GL-004)
Location: Wolfcamp Express 5H to Processing Facility
Equipment Type: Steel Pipeline Infrastructure
Condition: Good with Minor Defects
Operating Hours: Continuous Service - 15,623 hours
Technician: Erik Johansson
Date: March 22, 2024

WORK PERFORMED:
Conducted inline inspection using magnetic flux leakage (MFL) intelligent pig. Performed external coating survey and cathodic protection system evaluation. Excavated and inspected three anomalous areas identified during pig run. Replaced damaged coating at Station 12+45 and installed additional test points for cathodic protection monitoring. Pressure tested repaired sections at 1,125 PSI.

PARTS REPLACED:
- External coating patch kit (3M brand)
- Cathodic protection test station
- Pipeline marker posts (qty 4)
- Valve actuator seals at mainline valve MLV-003

TEST RESULTS:
Inline inspection revealed 12 minor metal loss features, all less than 10% wall thickness. Cathodic protection survey shows adequate protection with potentials ranging from -850 to -1,200 mV CSE. Pressure test on repaired section held steady at test pressure for 8 hours with zero pressure drop. Coating adhesion tests passed at all inspection points.

SAFETY OBSERVATIONS:
Right-of-way clear of encroachments. Warning signs and marker posts in good condition.

NEXT SERVICE RECOMMENDATION:
Next inline inspection due in 24 months. Monitor cathodic protection readings monthly. Schedule vegetation management for Q2 2024. Implement quarterly aerial patrol inspections. Consider upgrading to impressed current cathodic protection system in areas showing lower protection levels.

documents.title: Equipment Maintenance Report - 300 BBL Production Tank (Unit TNK-001)
documents.content:
EQUIPMENT MAINTENANCE REPORT

Equipment: 300 BBL Production Tank (Unit TNK-001)
Location: Gulf Stream 1
Equipment Type: Crude Oil Storage Tank
Condition: Fair - Coating Deterioration Noted
Operating Hours: 12,445 hours (continuous service)
Technician: Priya Patel
Date: March 25, 2024

WORK PERFORMED:
Performed comprehensive tank inspection including bottom scan, shell thickness measurements, and roof structural assessment. Cleaned and inspected flame arrestor and pressure/vacuum relief devices. Replaced corroded thief hatch gasket and repaired minor shell plate corrosion on east side of tank. Applied protective coating to prepared areas and tested all tank instrumentation systems.

PARTS REPLACED:
- Thief hatch assembly with new gasket
- Pressure/vacuum relief valve (6-inch)
- Tank gauging tape and float
- Flame arrestor element
- External ladder safety cage hardware

TEST RESULTS:
Ultrasonic thickness readings show minimum shell thickness of 0.187 inches (original: 0.250 inches). Bottom scan revealed no significant pitting or hole formation. Pressure test on vacuum relief valve successful at -2.5 PSI. Flame arrestor flow test passed at maximum design flow rate. Tank capacity calibration confirms 298.7 BBL usable volume.

SAFETY OBSERVATIONS:
Secondary containment integrity confirmed. Grounding system continuity verified. Emergency response equipment properly positioned.

NEXT SERVICE RECOMMENDATION:
Schedule full external coating renovation within 6 months due to accelerated corrosion noted on east shell plates. Replace tank bottom in 18 months based on thickness readings. Upgrade to electronic tank gauging system during next turnaround. Implement monthly ultrasonic thickness monitoring program for early corrosion detection.

traces.task: Evaluate whether to drill a new well in Bakken Formation formation at proposed location

steps.thought: Assess geological data and reservoir characteristics
steps.action: Query formation properties, nearby well results, and reservoir estimates
steps.observation: Seismic data shows the proposed location sits in a high-quality Middle Bakken interval with 45-foot net pay thickness and porosity averaging 12%, while offset wells within 2 miles have achieved initial production rates of 800-1,200 BOPD with estimated ultimate recovery of 650,000 BOE.

steps.thought: Review permit requirements and regulatory constraints
steps.action: Check permit status and environmental restrictions for the area
steps.observation: The drilling permit application is approved and environmental assessment shows no protected species or water sources within the 500-foot buffer zone, though North Dakota requires a $50,000 reclamation bond and quarterly groundwater monitoring for the first two years.

steps.thought: Evaluate economic viability based on estimated reserves
steps.action: Calculate projected production, costs, and breakeven price
steps.observation: Economic modeling indicates the well will cost $8.2 million to drill and complete with projected 18-month payback at current WTI prices of $78/barrel, generating an estimated NPV of $4.8 million and IRR of 35% over 15 years.

steps.thought: Assess operational risks and mitigation options
steps.action: Review inspection history and equipment availability for similar wells
steps.observation: Recent mechanical integrity tests on nearby wells show 98% success rate with no major equipment failures, and drilling contractor Nabors has two available rigs within 50 miles that can commence operations within 30 days.

traces.outcome: Proceed with drilling authorization based on strong geological indicators, regulatory compliance, and attractive economics that exceed the company's 25% IRR threshold. Secure rig scheduling with Nabors for Q2 spud date to optimize winter drilling conditions.

traces.task: Determine if well Bone Spring 2H requires workover or stimulation

steps.thought: Analyze production decline and compare to type curve
steps.action: Query production history and calculate decline rate
steps.observation: Production analysis shows Bone Spring 2H has declined from initial 950 BOE/d to current 185 BOE/d over 18 months, with decline rate accelerating to 3.2% monthly versus 2.1% type curve expectation. Water cut has increased from 15% to 47% indicating potential completion integrity issues.

steps.thought: Review well condition and recent inspection findings
steps.action: Check inspection reports and equipment condition assessments
steps.observation: Inspection reports reveal 15% scale buildup in tubing and minor corrosion on packers, but no critical equipment failures. Downhole pressure gauge shows reservoir pressure maintained at 3,200 psi, suggesting near-wellbore damage rather than reservoir depletion.

steps.thought: Evaluate intervention options and expected uplift
steps.action: Compare costs and production gains for workover vs stimulation vs status quo
steps.observation: Economic analysis indicates restimulation would cost $1.2M with projected 65% production uplift for 24-month payout, while workover costs $380K with 25% uplift for 18-month payout. Current well NPV at $2.1M supports intervention.

traces.outcome: Recommend proceeding with restimulation of Bone Spring 2H based on favorable economics and reservoir pressure maintenance, with operations scheduled for Q2 to optimize production before peak demand season.

traces.task: Assess whether to repair or replace Natco 3-Phase Separator at well Powder River 6H

steps.thought: Review equipment condition history and failure patterns
steps.action: Query inspection records and maintenance history
steps.observation: Inspection records show the Natco 3-Phase Separator has experienced three major seal failures in the past 18 months, with increasing frequency of minor repairs and corrosion detected in the oil weir compartment. The unit was manufactured in 2018 and has processed approximately 2.1 million barrels, operating at 85% efficiency compared to its original specification.

steps.thought: Compare repair cost vs replacement cost and downtime impact
steps.action: Estimate repair costs, replacement costs, and production loss
steps.observation: Repair costs are estimated at $78,000 including new internals and vessel modifications, with 5 days downtime resulting in $145,000 production loss at current 850 BOPD rate. Complete replacement with a new Natco unit would cost $285,000 plus $35,000 installation, requiring 7 days downtime but including a 3-year warranty.

steps.thought: Consider remaining useful life and technology upgrades
steps.action: Evaluate newer equipment options and efficiency improvements
steps.observation: Newer separator models offer 12% improved oil recovery efficiency and enhanced sand handling capabilities that would reduce downstream processing costs by an estimated $25,000 annually. The current unit has an estimated remaining useful life of 3-4 years if repaired, while a new unit would provide 15+ years of service.

traces.outcome: Recommend replacing the separator based on the analysis showing that while repair costs are lower upfront, the combination of improved efficiency, reduced maintenance, and extended service life of new equipment provides better long-term value with payback within 2.8 years.
